reasons: Hylos gives Irithel a moving frontline and pathway speed so she can keep repositioning while attacking.
Irithel benefits when Hylos slows enemies and keeps them away from her damage position.

proof.summary: Hylos creates space and movement support, while Irithel uses that space to keep mobile basic attacks active.
worksBestWhen: Hylos opens a path before Irithel commits forward.
Irithel stays behind Hylos's frontline pressure.
failureCases: Hylos cannot stay between Irithel and divers.
Enemy burst targets Irithel before the pathway matters.

reasons: Hylos can create a safer front and movement path for Layla.
Layla benefits from Hylos's frontline because she needs distance and protection to maintain backline damage.

proof.summary: Hylos protects and opens space, while Layla uses the safer path to maintain backline damage.
worksBestWhen: Hylos positions between Layla and enemy divers.
Layla uses the pathway to reposition instead of overextending.

reasons: Hylos can create a pathway and frontline disruption that helps Claude enter or exit during Blazing Duet.
Claude benefits when Hylos draws control and opens a safer teamfight route.

proof.summary: Hylos opens the teamfight route, while Claude uses the movement window to position for AoE damage.
worksBestWhen: Hylos starts the path before Claude commits.
Claude waits for Hylos to draw control first.
failureCases: Claude enters while enemy control is still ready.
Hylos's pathway does not reach the fight angle.
Claude lacks item timing for meaningful follow-up.

reasons: Hylos can stand forward and slow enemies, giving Pharsa safer time to cast from the backline.
Pharsa benefits when Hylos zones enemies away from her channeling or burst position.

proof.summary: Hylos controls the front and enemy movement, while Pharsa follows with ranged poke or burst.
worksBestWhen: Hylos zones enemies away from Pharsa.
Pharsa casts from a safe angle behind the frontline.
failureCases: Hylos cannot reach the enemy backline.
Enemies disengage outside Pharsa's damage area.

reasons: Hylos can slow and occupy enemies near the fight center, giving Odette a more stable area to apply AoE damage.
Odette benefits when Hylos absorbs pressure before she commits to her damage window.

proof.summary: Hylos keeps enemies pressured in the fight area, while Odette adds AoE magic damage from behind or beside him.
worksBestWhen: Hylos enters first and draws attention.
Odette casts after enemy control is partially spent.
failureCases: Odette is interrupted before damage finishes.
Hylos and Odette fight in separate zones.
Enemy burst ignores Hylos and dives Odette.
